_comment: Ground truth for Ali Hadi case #1 'Web Server' (SQLi -> webshells -> RDP).
Tier: YELLOW (build/test, score with care) — answers gated.
Disk + memory from one host: best fit for the cross-artifact correlation track.
A strong run corroborates the webshell across disk (file) and memory (process).
license: Ali Hadi DFIR challenges — free for research/education (answers gated)

findings.description: SQL injection attempts against the web application in server access logs
findings.artifact_hint: Web server access logs (IIS/Apache) with injection payloads

findings.description: Webshell written to the web root following exploitation
findings.artifact_hint: Suspicious script in wwwroot/htdocs; MFT creation time

findings.description: Webshell-spawned process as an anomalous child of the web server in memory
findings.artifact_hint: vol pslist/psscan: cmd/powershell child of w3wp/httpd

findings.description: Interactive RDP logon after the web compromise
New or elevated account created for persistence
Attacker tooling staged on disk post-compromise
findings.artifact_hint: Dropped binaries in temp/web paths; prefetch
